Iron Man 3 (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $408,992,272Details
International Box Office $805,638,684
Worldwide Box Office $1,214,630,956
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $30,288,394
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $56,844,398
Total $87,132,792
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Brash-but-brilliant industrialist Tony Stark/Iron Man is pitted against an enemy whose reach knows no bounds. When Stark finds his personal world destroyed at his enemy’s hands, he embarks on a harrowing quest to find those responsible. This journey, at every turn, will test his mettle. With his back against the wall, Stark is left to survive by his own devices, relying on his ingenuity and instincts to protect those closest to him. As he fights his way back, Stark discovers the answer to the question that has secretly haunted him: does the man make the suit or does the suit make the man?

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$174,144,585 (42.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.35 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:33.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$200,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,253 opening theaters/4,253 max. theaters, 6.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $601,666,374

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 3rd, 2013 (Wide) by Walt Disney
May 3rd, 2013 (IMAX) by Walt Disney

Synopsis

Brash-but-brilliant industrialist Tony Stark/Iron Man is pitted against an enemy whose reach knows no bounds. When Stark finds his personal world destroyed at his enemy’s hands, he embarks on a harrowing quest to find those responsible. This journey, at every turn, will test his mettle. With his back against the wall, Stark is left to survive by his own devices, relying on his ingenuity and instincts to protect those closest to him. As he fights his way back, Stark discovers the answer to the question that has secretly haunted him: does the man make the suit or does the suit make the man?

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$174,144,585 (42.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.35 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:33.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$200,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,253 opening theaters/4,253 max. theaters, 6.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $601,666,374
